title: Charmed Life - Chapter 20 (Fragment 1)
text:
WHO IS CHRESTOMANCI

P ERFECT B OUND E-BOOK EXCLUSIVE EXTRAS

W HO IS C RESTOMANCI

WHO IS CHRESTOMANCI ? The question should really be What is Chrestomanci ? For, as was revealed in Charmed Life , Chrestomanci is actually the title of a job and not a person’s name at all. The Chrestomanci is employed by the Government to monitor and control the use of magic throughout the related worlds.

This is not the most popular of jobs, especially with the less-than-scrupulous characters who tend to be tempted by any opportunity to misuse power, and particularly if it can earn them lots of money or an exciting life. But using magic is always dangerous, and it always has consequences, so even the best-intentioned witch or warlock needs help and guidance from time to time. Not only that, but there are plenty of ordinary people in the worlds without even a whiff of magical ability, and they need someone to make sure that the magic users don’t get their own way all of the time — which is where Chrestomanci comes in.

As well as looking after people, Chrestomanci also has to protect the worlds they live in. Many spells need certain ingredients to work properly, just like recipes — which is fine if they are items like six drops of dew from a yew tree at dawn, or a handful of grass from inside a stone circle, or even dried mouse droppings. But some spells call for substances such as dragon’s blood or mermaid scales. These are illegal products, because of the potential harm caused by both the collection and careless use of such dark materials. But this also means there is a thriving black market in “exotic supplies,” so Chrestomanci and his team have to be constantly on the alert for smugglers and underhand dealers.

Of course, not everyone is qualified to take on the post of Chrestomanci — it’s not a job you can simply apply for. Only the strength and special skills of an enchanter can possibly begin to attempt to tackle the task. But Chrestomanci is no ordinary enchanter — he has to be a nine-lifed enchanter. Most people, whatever their magical status, just have one life each — but they also have counterparts, or doubles, in other worlds. People with nine lives (and they are exceedingly rare) exist only in their own world, with no counterparts anywhere else, which means that all the talents and energies and abilities that would have been spread out across the others are all concentrated in the one person. This makes Chrestomanci a very powerful enchanter, indeed.

vocabulary.definition: To make a formal request or seek to be considered for a position or job.
vocabulary.example: One cannot simply apply for the post of Chrestomanci.

vocabulary.definition: Of the greatest importance; principal or essential.
vocabulary.example: Words central to the fragment.

vocabulary.definition: The title of a job responsible for monitoring and controlling magic across related worlds.
vocabulary.example: The Chrestomanci is employed by the Government to monitor and control the use of magic.

vocabulary.definition: To gather together; to focus all efforts or talents into one area or person.
vocabulary.example: All abilities are concentrated in the one person with nine lives.

vocabulary.definition: A result or effect of an action or condition.
vocabulary.example: Using magic always has consequences.

vocabulary.definition: A person or thing that corresponds to or has the same function as another in a different place or situation.
vocabulary.example: People have counterparts or doubles in other worlds.

vocabulary.definition: To make use of; to engage the services of a person or thing.
vocabulary.example: The Chrestomanci is employed by the Government to monitor magic.

vocabulary.definition: A person who performs magic or enchantments.
vocabulary.example: Only an enchanter with special skills can be Chrestomanci.

vocabulary.definition: Originating in or characteristic of a distant foreign country; unusual or striking.
vocabulary.example: Exotic supplies attract smugglers.

vocabulary.definition: Advice or information aimed at resolving a problem or difficulty.
vocabulary.example: Witches and warlocks need guidance from time to time.

vocabulary.definition: Forbidden by law or statute.
vocabulary.example: Dragon’s blood is an illegal product due to its potential harm.

vocabulary.definition: A component part or element of a mixture or recipe.
vocabulary.example: Six drops of dew are an ingredient in a spell.

vocabulary.definition: To use something in an incorrect or harmful way.
vocabulary.example: Some people misuse their magical powers for personal gain.

vocabulary.definition: To observe and check the progress or quality of something over a period.
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci monitors the use of magic throughout the worlds.

vocabulary.definition: A position of employment or office.
vocabulary.example: The post of Chrestomanci requires special skills.

vocabulary.definition: Having or showing the capacity to develop into something in the future.
vocabulary.example: Potential harm caused by careless use of dark materials.

vocabulary.definition: Having great power or strength.
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci is a very powerful enchanter.

vocabulary.definition: Officially recognized as being trained to perform a particular job.
vocabulary.example: Not everyone is qualified to take on the post of Chrestomanci.

vocabulary.definition: Not occurring very often; uncommon.
vocabulary.example: Nine-lives people are exceedingly rare.

vocabulary.definition: Diligent, thorough, and extremely attentive to details or morals.
vocabulary.example: Less-than-scrupulous characters tend to misuse power.

vocabulary.definition: A person who illegally imports or exports goods.
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci must be alert for smugglers.

vocabulary.definition: A form of words used as a magical charm or incantation.
vocabulary.example: Many spells need certain ingredients to work properly.

vocabulary.definition: A piece of work to be done or undertaken.
vocabulary.example: The task of monitoring magic is difficult.

vocabulary.definition: Flourishing or growing vigorously; successful.
vocabulary.example: There is a thriving black market in exotic supplies.

vocabulary.definition: Acting or done in a secret or dishonest way.
vocabulary.example: Underhand dealers traffic illegal magical supplies.

vocabulary.definition: A slight smell or trace of something.
vocabulary.example: Ordinary people without a whiff of magical ability.
